label: clipboard-translate-builtin
summary: User wants to translate clipboard text. Builtin source, hotkey trigger, amber color.

turns.content: 我想做一个键帽，按一下把剪贴板里的文字翻译成正式英文邮件。

好。先确认两件事：要叫什么名字？默认我用 "Clipboard Translate"；快捷键 Ctrl+Shift+T 可以吗？
<keycap-slot field="name">Clipboard Translate</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="id">clipboard-translate</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="description">Translate clipboard text into formal English email tone.</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="icon">🌐</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="keycap_color">amber</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="author.handle">local-creator</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="version">0.1.0</keycap-slot>

label: feishu-meeting-summary-oauth
summary: User wants to summarize their latest Feishu meeting. OAuth source for Feishu, pool trigger, jade color.

turns.content: I want a keycap that summarises my latest Feishu meeting transcript and writes the summary back to a Feishu Docs page.

Got it. Calling it "Feishu Meeting Recap" — OK? It will live in the Pool (no hotkey by default; you can add one later).
<keycap-slot field="name">Feishu Meeting Recap</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="id">feishu-meeting-recap</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="description">Summarise the latest Feishu meeting transcript and append the summary to a Feishu Docs page.</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="icon">📝</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="keycap_color">jade</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="author.handle">local-creator</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="version">0.1.0</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="source">{"type":"oauth","vendor":"feishu","oauth_config":{"auth_url":"https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/authen/v1/index","token_url":"https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/authen/v2/oauth/token","scopes":["docx:document","vc:meeting:readonly"],"client_id_env":"FEISHU_CLIENT_ID"},"api_calls":[{"name":"list_recent_meetings","method":"GET","path":"/open-apis/vc/v1/meetings"},{"name":"get_transcript","method":"GET","path":"/open-apis/vc/v1/meetings/{meeting_id}/transcript"},{"name":"append_doc","method":"POST","path":"/open-apis/docx/v1/documents/{doc_id}/blocks"}]}</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="triggers">[{"kind":"pool_select","section":"middle","rank":70}]</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="capabilities">[{"OAuthAccess":{"vendor":"feishu","scopes":["docx:document","vc:meeting:readonly"]}},{"LlmCall":{"model":"doubao-1-5-pro-256k","max_tokens":4096}}]</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="flow">{"initial":"idle","states":{"idle":{"on_event":{"invoked":{"target":"fetching"}}},"fetching":{"on_enter":[{"OAuthCall":{"name":"list_recent_meetings"}},{"OAuthCall":{"name":"get_transcript"}}],"on_event":{"transcript_ready":{"target":"summarising"}}},"summarising":{"on_enter":[{"LlmCall":{"prompt_template":"meeting-summary-bullets"}}],"on_event":{"llm_response":{"target":"writing"}}},"writing":{"on_enter":[{"OAuthCall":{"name":"append_doc"}}],"on_event":{"appended":{"target":"done"}}},"done":{}}}</keycap-slot>
<keycap-ready/>

label: screenshot-ocr-builtin
summary: Take a region screenshot, OCR it, copy text to clipboard. Builtin source, hotkey trigger, platinum color.

turns.content: Screenshot a region and OCR it to the clipboard. Bind to Ctrl+Shift+O.

Calling it "Screenshot OCR".
<keycap-slot field="name">Screenshot OCR</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="id">screenshot-ocr</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="description">Capture a screen region, OCR the image, place the text on the clipboard.</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="icon">📸</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="keycap_color">platinum</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="author.handle">local-creator</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="version">0.1.0</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="source">{"type":"builtin","module":"screenshot-ocr"}</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="triggers">[{"kind":"hotkey","combo":"Ctrl+Shift+O","contexts":["anywhere"]},{"kind":"pool_select","section":"middle","rank":40}]</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="capabilities">["ScreenCapture","ClipboardWrite",{"LlmCall":{"model":"doubao-vision","max_tokens":2048}}]</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="flow">{"initial":"idle","states":{"idle":{"on_event":{"invoked":{"target":"capturing"}}},"capturing":{"on_enter":["ScreenCapture"],"on_event":{"capture_done":{"target":"recognising"}}},"recognising":{"on_enter":[{"LlmCall":{"prompt_template":"image-ocr"}}],"on_event":{"llm_response":{"target":"writing"}}},"writing":{"on_enter":["ClipboardWrite"],"on_event":{"clipboard_written":{"target":"done"}}},"done":{}}}</keycap-slot>
<keycap-ready/>

label: mcp-postgres-query
summary: Wrap an existing local Postgres MCP server. MCP source, pool trigger, graphite color (advanced).

turns.content: I already run a postgres MCP server locally on stdio. Make me a keycap that lets me run read-only queries against the production replica.

Got it. Tagging it as advanced (graphite), pool-only trigger.
<keycap-slot field="name">Postgres Query</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="id">postgres-query</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="description">Run read-only SQL against the production Postgres replica via the local postgres MCP server.</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="icon">🐘</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="keycap_color">graphite</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="author.handle">local-creator</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="version">0.1.0</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="source">{"type":"mcp","server":"postgres","tools":["query","describe"],"auto_install":false}</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="triggers">[{"kind":"pool_select","section":"bottom","rank":10}]</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="capabilities">[{"McpInvoke":{"server":"postgres","tool_glob":"query_*"}}]</keycap-slot>
<keycap-slot field="flow">{"initial":"idle","states":{"idle":{"on_event":{"invoked":{"target":"awaiting_query"}}},"awaiting_query":{"on_event":{"query_submitted":{"target":"executing"}}},"executing":{"on_enter":[{"McpCall":{"server":"postgres","tool":"query"}}],"on_event":{"mcp_result":{"target":"done"}}},"done":{}}}</keycap-slot>
<keycap-ready/>

label: patch-after-ready
summary: User reviews the manifest and asks to rename the keycap. Single-field patch flow.

turns.content: (prior turn ended with <keycap-ready/>; manifest already shows id=clipboard-translate, name=Clipboard Translate)

Renamed. Id stays kebab-cased to match.
<keycap-patch field="name">Quick Translate</keycap-patch>
<keycap-patch field="id">quick-translate</keycap-patch>

label: emit-manifest-response
summary: PWA prompts with <emit-manifest/> after <keycap-ready/>; Irisy outputs only the two code blocks.
